title: Occupational Health Physician - based in Luxembourg
description:
**This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence.** 
 The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support.
   

  

 The
 **EIB** 
 , the European Union's bank, is seeking to recruit for its Human Resources Directorate (HR), Occupational Health Service Unit (OHS) at its headquarters in Luxembourg, an
 **Occupational Health Physician\*.**
**This is a full-time temporary position at grade 5/6. Part-time can be considered with a minimum of 60%.**
**The term of this contract will be 15th December 2026;**
* Internal benchmark Officer Human Resources


***Panel interviews are anticipated for May/June.***
 Purpose
   

  

 In this role of Occupational Health Physician, you will monitor the health and safety of staff members by conducting internal occupational health assessments. There will also be an opportunity to contribute to prevention and promotion of health at work initiatives and developing policies and actions as well as providing recommendations for HR management in order to fulfil the Bank’s duty of care. In this role, you may be called upon to advise on and help develop policies on Organisational Health, particularly in the domain of psychosocial and occupational risks.
   

  

 Operating Network
   

  

 You will report to the Head of the Occupational Health Service Unit.
   

  

 You will have regular contact with all staff and might interact also with external contacts (Hospitals & Clinics, Laboratories, External Doctors, other EU Institutions).
   

  

 The work requires on site presence as it will be carried out at EIB premises.
   

  

 Accountabilities
   

  

**Your Main Duties Will Include**
* Carry out internal occupational health assessments and reintegration medical assessments.
* Identify, prevent and monitor occupational risks, with a focus on psychosocial risks and mental health issues.
* Execute pre-employment medical assessments.
* Maintain the standards of accurate and complete recording and reporting on the health services’ activities.


**Subject To Capacity / Availability, Additional Tasks May Include**
* Carry out Fitness for Travel assessments in order to fulfil the Bank’s commitments under the Overseas Security and Health Policy Provide Travel Health advice to staff going on missions in order to ensure a timely and efficient delivery of responses and provide excellent customer service.
* Provide emergency care to EIB Group staff.
* Identify, prevent and monitor occupational health problems.
* Advise managers and HR staff members regarding medical cases.
* Contribute to the development, implementation and communication of policies and procedures in occupational health and support the implementation of plans and initiatives in particular: prevention and health promotion programmes, stress, musculoskeletal and injury prevention, first aid, health education, public health epidemiology incl. handling of pandemics, smoking cessation, weight control, and screening for cardiovascular and other risks.
* Liaise with the responsible services in the Bank when medical adjustments are required (i.e. in case the staff member has temporarily or permanently reduced mobility).


**Qualifications**
* Qualified physician in general medicine.
* At least 5 years of relevant experience, ideally in the field of occupational health. Specialization in psychosocial risk prevention would be a distinct advantage.
* Good knowledge of mental health emergency response protocols.
* Good knowledge of the administrative policies and procedures governing the management of medical files.
* Experience in working in an administration would be a plus.
* Experience in the development of health policies and/or programmes would be advantageous.
* Good knowledge of travel medicine and immunization would be a plus.
* Respect of the absolute confidentiality of personal health related data and discretion.
* Interpersonal sensitivity.
* Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*\*\*), with a good command of the other. (Knowledge of other EU languages would be an advantage).


 Competencies
   

  

**Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here**
 To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here
   

  

*(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of* 
*level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.*
 We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity.
   

  

 If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately.
   

  

 By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation.
   

  

 Deadline for applications:
 **8th May 2026**

title: Modellägare Transaktionsmonitorering LF Finans
description:
*Vill du ta ansvar för hur transaktionsmonitorering fungerar i en verksamhet med egna produkter, kundflöden och riskmönster? Vi söker nu en Modellägare Transaktionsmonitorering till Länsförsäkringar Bank – en nyckelroll inom Financial Crime Prevention (FCP), där du arbetar med fokus på LF Finans. Här får du kombinera ett helhetsansvar för modell och analys med en nära koppling till affären och dess specifika förutsättningar där förståelse för produkter, kundflöden och affärslogisk är avgörande.*
**Om jobbet**
 I rollen som Modellägare Transaktionsmonitorering är du en del av FCP, men arbetar med särskilt fokus på LF Finans – vårt dotterbolag med egna produkter, kundbeteenden och transaktionsflöden. Det ställer höga krav på din förmåga att förstå affären och omsätta den förståelsen till träffsäkra modeller.
   

  

 Som Modellägare Transaktionsmonitorering har du ett övergripande ansvar för monitoreringen inom transaktionsövervakningen, med ansvar för modellens träffsäkerhet och kontinuerliga utveckling. Du analyserar mönster och beteenden kopplade till LF Finans affär och omsätter affärs- och riskbehov till effektiva, datadrivna lösningar. Du arbetar nära verksamheten inom LF Finans, liksom AML-analytiker och specialister, verksamhetsutvecklare, IT och systemleverantör. Rollen innebär att du är drivande i att utveckla och optimera modeller och arbetssätt i en miljö där både affär och riskbild utvecklas löpande.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer exempelvis att vara:
   

  

* Ansvara för monitoreringen inom transaktionsövervakningen med fokus på LF Finans
* Analysera transaktionsmönster, kundbeteenden och regelutfall
* Sätta, utvärdera och utveckla regler och modeller baserat på data och riskbedömningar
* Följa upp modellens träffsäkerhet och identifiera förbättringsområden
* Driva kontinuerliga förbättringar utifrån dataanalys och förändrade modus
* Samarbeta nära verksamheten inom LF Finans samt med AML-specialister, IT och leverantörer
* Ta fram rutiner och arbetsprocesser för området


 Vi söker dig som har:
   

  

* Akademisk examen inom ett relevant område
* Minst 3 års erfarenhet av liknande uppdrag av arbete med dataanalys, modeller eller regelverk
* Ett datadrivet arbetssätt och stark analytisk förmåga
* Kompetens inom programmering och modellering
* Erfarenhet från arbete inom AML och FCP‑området
* God förståelse för finansiella produkter och den marknad där LF Finans verkar


 Meriterande är även om du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet från arbete nära affären, exempelvis i kundorganisation inom bank eller finans


 Som person har du en mycket god samarbetsförmåga och trivs i gränslandet mellan verksamhet och teknik. Du har hög kognitiv förmåga och trivs i en roll där du behöver fatta välgrundade beslut baserat på komplex information. Vidare är du strukturerad och noggrann och kan omsätta komplexa data till tydliga insikter och rekommendationer. Du tar gärna initiativ, driver utveckling och får med dig andra även utan formellt mandat.
   

  

**Kontaktpersoner:**
 Rekryterande chef: tina.govers@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Forena: jessica.loow@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

 Facklig representant Akademikerföreningen: christina.berg@lansforsakringar.se
   

  

*Låter det spännande? Då vill vi veta mer om dig! Välkommen med din ansökan redan idag. Vi arbetar löpande med denna rekrytering och kan komma att tillsätta tjänsten innan sista ansökningsdag.*
 Länsförsäkringar Bank är en av Sveriges största banker med ett brett erbjudande inom betalningar, sparande och bolån.
   

  

 Financial Crime Prevention (FCP) är ett prioriterat område där vi stärker vårt arbete med att förebygga, identifiera och hantera finansiell brottslighet. Vi är på en resa där vi bygger framtidens förmågor inom området – med fokus på datadrivna arbetssätt, effektiv regelefterlevnad och ett starkt kundskydd. Här får du arbeta i en verksamhet med hög relevans, där ditt arbete bidrar till att skydda både kunder och samhälle. Hos oss blir du en del av en miljö där vi tillsammans utvecklar lösningar, skapar struktur i komplexa frågor och formar framtidens landskap – med målet att skapa trygghet och möjligheter.

title: Chief Technology Officer (CTO)
description:
Sensys Gatso is a Swedish publicly listed company with a global presence specializing in traffic safety solutions. Our technology portfolio spans hardware, software, and comprehensive services designed to enhance road safety and efficiency worldwide. As a trusted industry leader, we are committed to innovation and operational excellence in delivering complex, integrated solutions to our diverse client base. Our mission is to save lives by positively changing driver behaviour.

**Position Summary**


The Chief Technology Officer (CTO) is a member of Sensys Gatso Group Management reporting directly to the Group CEO. The position is a key executive leadership role, responsible for defining, refining, and executing the company's global technology strategy, roadmaps, and architectural vision. The CTO will oversee all technology disciplines—including product development, engineering (hardware and software), and IT infrastructure—ensuring alignment with the company's business objectives, driving innovation, and maintaining technological competitive advantage. This role requires a proven leader with deep technical subject matter expertise and exceptional resource management skills, capable of steering a multi-disciplinary technology organization.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Technology Strategy & Vision**

* Define and communicate a clear, long-term technology strategy and roadmap that supports the company’s growth, global expansion, and business goals.
* Lead the identification, evaluation, and integration of new emerging technologies relevant to traffic enforcement, smart cities, and integrated hardware/software/service solutions.
* Drive innovation across product lines, ensuring our solutions remain cutting-edge and meet evolving market demands.

**Leadership & Management**

* Provide executive leadership and mentorship to the global Product, Engineering (Hardware and Software), and IT teams.
* Manage technology budgets, resources, and staffing to ensure efficient delivery of projects and strategic initiatives.
* Foster a culture of technical excellence, accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.

**Product & Engineering Execution**

* Oversee the entire product lifecycle management (PLM) process, from ideation and R&D through development, deployment, and end-of-life.
* Direct the combined engineering efforts for integrated hardware, software, and service solutions, ensuring seamless and robust delivery.
* Guide the development teams in implementing best practices in software development, architecture, and quality assurance.
* Ensure excellence in hardware engineering, focusing on reliability, manufacturing efficiency, and future-proofing the physical technology base.

**IT, Security, and Compliance**

* Oversee IT infrastructure and systems (both internal operations and client-facing solutions), including management of on-premises, cloud, and hybrid environments.
* Establish and enforce world-class cybersecurity protocols and compliance frameworks (e.g., GDPR, local regulatory requirements) across all products, services, and internal systems.
* Ensure operational resilience, data integrity, and high availability of critical technology systems.

**Cross-Functional Collaboration**

* Partner closely with the Group’s Extended Management Team to translate business objectives into technology initiatives.
* Collaborate with Sales and Operations to ensure successful deployment and servicing of technology solutions to clients globally.**Experience and Subject Matter Expertise**The ideal candidate will have significant experience and subject matter expertise across all the following disciplines:**Hardware Engineering**Proven experience leading the design, development, testing, and manufacturing/assembly of complex, specialized electronic and mechanical hardware components.**Software Development**Extensive background in leading large-scale software development, including cloud-native architectures, embedded systems, data processing, and modern development methodologies (e.g., Agile, DevOps).**IT Infrastructure & Systems**Deep knowledge of managing global IT infrastructure, including enterprise systems, network architecture, and both on-premises and significant cloud deployment models for internal and client-facing services.**Cybersecurity and Compliance**Expertise in developing and implementing robust security strategies, data protection standards, and navigating international regulatory compliance mandates.**Product Lifecycle Management (PLM)**Experience in managing technology products from concept through market launch, continuous iteration, and retirement, optimizing for profitability and customer satisfaction.**Integrated Solutions**Demonstrated ability to successfully integrate and deliver combined hardware, software, and service solutions in a complex B2G or B2B environment.**R&D Leadership**Track record of leading R&D teams, managing innovation portfolios, and successfully transitioning nascent technologies into commercially viable products.**Qualifications**
* Minimum of 10 years of progressive experience in technology leadership roles, with at least 5 years as a CTO, VP of Engineering, or equivalent senior technology executive.
* A master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field is preferred.
* Proven ability to operate effectively within a European business context and manage global technology operations.
* Exceptional communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical issues to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, including the Board of Directors.
* Fluency in English is required; proficiency in Swedish or Dutch is preferred.
* Residency in Sweden or the Netherlands is preferred.

title: Senior DevOps-arkitekt (konsult) - inom försvars- och säkerhetsindustrin
description:
Vi söker nu en senior DevOps-arkitekt till ett konsultuppdrag hos vår kund inom försvars- och säkerhetsindustrin. Uppdraget är centralt för att skapa rätt förutsättningar för utvecklingsteam genom moderna arbetssätt, robust infrastruktur och genomtänkta principer för datahantering.
  
Om uppdragetI rollen blir du en del av ett DevOps-team där du kombinerar ett arkitektansvar med ett operativt arbete som senior DevOps-ingenjör. Du arbetar nära utvecklingsteamen och bidrar med struktur, riktlinjer och tekniska lösningar som möjliggör effektiv, skalbar och hållbar mjukvaruutveckling.
  
Du är med och etablerar samt vidareutvecklar arbetssätt inom DevOps, med fokus på hur mjukvara byggs, integreras och förändras på ett modernt och kvalitetssäkrat sätt. En viktig del av uppdraget är också att säkerställa att datahantering och dataprinciper integreras tidigt i utvecklingsprocessen.
  
Ansvarsområden
  
Stötta utvecklingsteam inom infrastruktur, verktyg och utvecklingsprocesser
  
Driva och vidareutveckla arbetssätt inom IaC, CaC och CI/CD
  
Bidra både i systemering och mjukvaruutveckling
  
Säkerställa struktur och riktlinjer för effektiv DevOps-leverans
  
Agera coach och mentor till mer juniora kollegor
  
Bidra till ett samarbetsinriktat och kunskapsdelande team
  
  

Krav
  
Senior erfarenhet inom DevOps, gärna i en arkitekt- eller ledande roll
  
Djup kunskap inom DevOps-principer och arbetssätt
  
**Erfarenhet av:** Infrastructure as Code (IaC)
  
Configuration as Code (CaC)
  
CI/CD
  
  

Erfarenhet av datahantering och att etablera dataprinciper tidigt i projekt
  
Förmåga att arbeta både med systemering och mjukvaruutveckling
  
Erfarenhet av att stötta och coacha utvecklingsteam
  
Mycket god kommunikativ förmåga
  
Hög grad av självledarskap
  
Samarbetsvillig och ödmjuk i sitt arbetssätt
  
  

Låter detta som din nästa utmaning?
  
Vad kul! Skicka in din ansökan så hör vi av oss för ett nästa steg i processen.
  
Som konsult hos oss på Job Solution Consulting omfattas man av en trygg anställning i nära dialog med sin konsultchef för att alltid säkerställa att du trivs och utvecklas i din roll. Vi erbjuder en rad förmåner med fokus på kompetensutveckling och välmående, Och självklart omfattas man också av kollektivavtal i sin tjänst hos oss. För eventuella frågor eller funderingar är du välkommen att höra av dig till mig.
  
Du når mig på fredrik.hovstadius@jobsolution.se.

title: Platform Engineer
description:
Är du redo att forma framtidens IT tillsammans med några av branschens vassaste konsulter? Accelerate växer och vi söker fler Platform Engineers som vill vara med och bygga smarta, hållbara och säkra lösningar i teknikens absoluta framkant.
  
Din roll Som en del av vårt DevOps-team får du arbeta med marknadsledande kunder och hjälpa dem genom deras digitala transformation. Du jobbar långsiktigt i teamleveranser med modernisering, automation och agil utveckling, enligt vår metodik Journey to Agile. Några av de tekniker/metoder som vi arbetar med är Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, Jenkins, Ansible, Git, Bash, Python.
  
Vem är du? Vi söker dig som har gedigen erfarenhet inom Plattform/DevOps och som brinner för automatisering och att bygga effektiva lösningar ute hos våra kunder. Du uttrycker dig obehindrat på svenska och engelska i både tal och skrift.
  
För denna position krävs svenskt medborgarskap, vi utför även bakgrundskontroll av slutkandidat.
  
  

**Du bör ha koll på:**  

Linux i enterprise-miljöer
  
  

**Containerteknik:** Docker, Kubernetes
  
  

**Script/programmering:** Bash, Python, Go
  
  

**Versionshantering:** Git
  
  

**Automatisering:** Ansible, Terraform
  
  

CI/CD och GitOps
  
  

**Extra plus om du även har koll på:**  

OpenStack
  
  

Public cloud
  
  

OpenShift/Rancher
  
  

HashiCorp Vault/Consul
  
  

Vi erbjuder dig Som anställd på Accelerate erbjuder vi förutom spännande uppdrag och tekniker:
  
  

Utbildning & certifieringar via våra partners (Red Hat, HashiCorp, GitLab m.fl.)
  
  

En trygg och säker anställning med bra förmåner
  
  

Kollektivavtal, friskvårdsbidrag och sjukvårdsförsäkring
  
  

Flexibilitet, frihet och balans mellan jobb och fritid
  
  

Att bli en del i ett passionerat team med tekniskt vassa kollegor
  
  

Vi tror på gemenskap och kompetensutbyte – här utvecklas vi tillsammans och stöttar varandra för att alltid ligga i framkant.
  
Placeringsort Accelerate finns på flertal orter i Sverige och Norge. För denna tjänst utgår du från vårt kontor i Göteborg, med möjlighet till hybridarbete.
  
Vi intervjuar löpande – vänta inte med att skicka in din ansökan!
  
Önskad start är snarast eller enligt överenskommelse.
  
Har du frågor? Kontakta Jesper Carlsson på jesper.carlsson@iver.se
  
Vi ser fram emot att träffa dig. Välkommen till Accelerate!

title: Cloud & Site Reliability Engineer
description:
Cloud & Site Reliability Engineer


Do you thrive on building robust and scalable cloud platforms that power modern SaaS solutions? Here’s your chance to shape the future of tool management technologies in a role where reliability and security are key. At Sandvik and Tool Flow Soultions, we offer an innovative and collaborative environment where your expertise truly matters.

**Why join us?**

* Be part of a forward-looking global team driving digital transformation
* Work hands-on with modern engineering practices, SRE, and DevSecOps
* Make a real impact on scalability, security, and customer value

**Your mission**


You join our Cloud Tech organization and play a critical role in scaling a secure and reliable SaaS Tool Management platform. Reporting directly to the Head of Cloud, you design and evolve cloud infrastructure, enable teams through modern practices, and drive operational excellence across multiple products.


What you’ll do:

* Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines with Azure DevOps and GitHub Actions, including reusable templates and automated quality checks
* Design, manage, and optimize Azure infrastructure across compute, networking, identity, and storage using Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform and Bicep)
* Apply SRE principles to enhance observability, reliability, and incident response processes
* Integrate DevSecOps practices such as security scanning, vulnerability management, and GDPR compliance into pipelines
* Support product teams with self-service capabilities and ensure stable, cost-efficient operations


This role is based in Stockholm, Sweden or Tübingen, Germany.

**Your profile**


You’ve got a strong foundation in cloud engineering, DevSecOps, and reliability practices. You confidently work with Microsoft Azure, understand cloud-native architectures, and know how to enable teams with the right tools and processes.


You bring:

* Solid experience with CI/CD pipelines and secure delivery workflows across GitHub and Azure DevOps
* Expertise in Infrastructure-as-Code with Terraform and Bicep
* Familiarity with SRE practices, observability tools, and operational reliability methods
* Knowledge of backend development with languages such as C#/.NET, Node.js, or Go
* Strong grasp of security automation and regulatory compliance frameworks


You’re analytical and detail-oriented, yet pragmatic in driving improvements. You communicate clearly, collaborate seamlessly, and stay curious to keep learning new tools and methods. You’re comfortable taking responsibility and enjoy working in an agile, team-oriented environment.

**Our culture**


At Sandvik, we’re tech driven, innovative and entrepreneurial. We believe that success is a team effort so we value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive culture where people can be themselves and reach their full potential. So, we invest in supporting each other, learning together and celebrating our differences. Visit our stories hub, LinkedIn or Facebook to get to know us further.

**Contact information**


For further information about this position, please contact Patrik Grönman, hiring manager, patrik.gronman@sandvik.com


We’ve already decided on which advertising channels and marketing campaigns we wish to use and respectfully decline any additional contacts in that matter.


Union contacts – Sweden

* Thomas Lilja, Unionen, +46 (0)70 261 04 82
* Tord Engström, Akademikerföreningen, +46 (0)76 106 62 87
* Peter Olsson-Andrée, Ledarna, +46 (0)70 222 48 55


Recruitment Specialist: Gustaf Sjögren

**How to apply**


Send your application no later than 3rd of May, 2026. Click apply and include your resume and cover letter in English. Please note that we don’t accept applications by e-mail. Job ID: R0091146.


To learn more about our recruitment process, please visit our career site or contact HR Services at hrservices.sweden@sandvik.com.


*Business area Machining is a global leading manufacturer of tools and tooling systems for advanced industrial metal cutting, as well as metal powder. In 2025, sales were approximately SEK 44 billion with about 18,700 employees.*

Din roll


Som systemadministratör med fokus på Azure DevOps Server (ADO) [f.d. TFS] kommer du att ansvara för att administrera och optimera vår Azure DevOps Server-miljö, inklusive att stödja våra utvecklingsteam med CI/CD-pipelines, versionshantering och byggprocesser. Du kommer att arbeta med att felsöka och lösa tekniska problem relaterade till ADO och integrerade system, samtidigt som du automatiserar och effektiviserar arbetsflöden för att höja produktiviteten. En viktig del av rollen är också att samarbeta med andra IT-funktioner för att säkerställa stabila, säkra och väl fungerande lösningar.


Din profil


Du har förmåga att arbeta både självständigt och i team, och behärskar flytande svenska samt har goda kunskaper i engelska samt erfarenhet av system och applikationsförvaltning.


Vi söker dig som har:

* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av administration och konfiguration av Azure DevOps Server (ADO)

Meriterande:

* Ärendehantering
* CI/CD-pipelines

* Application Life Management (ALM) – t.ex. rådgivning inom vilken versionshantering som passar Git eller Team Foundation Version Control (TFVC)
* Kunna använda sig av Azure DevOps Server API:er


Befattningen kräver att du genomgår och godkänns enligt vid var tid gällande bestämmelser för säkerhetsskydd. För befattningar där Saab har krav på säkerhetsklassinplacering kan, i förekommande fall, medföra krav på visst medborgarskap.


Vad du blir en del av


Upptäck oändliga möjligheter. Anta utmaningar, skapa smarta innovationer och uppgradera dina förmågor. Det här är en plats för kluriga tänkare, modiga pionjärer och alla däremellan. Tillsammans uppnår vi det extraordinära, var och en tar med sitt unika perspektiv. Varje bidrag räknas.


Saab är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med uppdrag att hjälpa nationer att skydda sin befolkning och bidrar till trygghet för människor och samhällen. Med 26,100 talangfulla medarbetare utvecklar Saab teknik och lösningar för en säkrare och mer hållbar värld.


Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här

title: Manager to SiNIX Flow
description:
SiNIX is growing and we are looking for a Manager for SiNIX Flow who wants to combine business development, recruitment, and people leadership in a fast-moving consulting environment. This role is for someone who enjoys working close to both customers and people — building strong teams while driving business forward.


Together with our clients, we build high-performing teams within software development, platform engineering, and modern infrastructure. Internally, we are shaping SiNIX Flow as a business area where growth, quality, and culture go hand in hand — guided by our vision to deliver world-class Flow AI solutions that enable our partners to achieve excellence.

 **Your Role**  

As a Manager at SiNIX Flow, you take a central role in building and developing our consulting business. You will work across sales, recruitment, and consultant leadership — with the opportunity to shape the role based on your strengths.


At its core, this is a role for someone who wants to take ownership of a segment, build relationships, and ensure that both clients and consultants succeed.


You will work with:

* Driving sales and building long-term client relationships
* Understanding customer needs and translating them into team and competence needs
* Recruiting and onboarding new consultants
* Leading and developing consultants (performance, growth, well-being)
* Managing consultant assignments, follow-ups, and overall delivery quality
* Working with forecasting, planning, and basic personnel administrative processes and tasks
* Structuring, documenting, and continuously improving how we work
* Contributing to the overall growth and direction of SiNIX Flow

**What We Look For**  

We believe you have a technical foundation combined with experience from roles where you have handled complex and multifaceted responsibilities, might have been as an Account Manager, Consultant Manager, Business Manager, Product owner/Project Manager, Technical lead or Team lead.


We expect:

* A couple of years’ experience from a leadership role (formal or informal)
* Experience working in dynamic environments, preferably from smaller companies or consulting businesses
* A technical background within software development, infrastructure, integration, CI/CD, test automation, platform engineering, devops or similar
* Experience in recruitment and/or sales
* Ability to understand both business needs and technical contexts


Meriting:

* Existing network within relevant industries

 **Who are you**  

You are an entrepreneurial and action-oriented person who enjoys having many things in motion at the same time. You combine a strong drive with a big-picture mindset — understanding how people, business, and delivery connect.  

You bring structure to complexity, you document, communicate clearly and transparently, and ensure alignment through continuous follow-up and visibility. You are a prestige-less team player who is comfortable rolling up your sleeves, supporting others, and doing what is needed for the team to succeed.

 **About SiNIX**  

At SiNIX, we prioritize the value of our colleagues by creating memorable experiences and sharing skills. We empower our employees with choices in training, vacation time, and flexible salaries, fostering a supportive and thriving work environment. Our aim is to inspire ongoing growth and development, making success a shared journey.

title: Enabling Engineer to Lindex
description:
Apply now


**Do you enjoy making technology easier to use, more reliable, and more empowering for others? At Lindex Technology, we’re on a journey to strengthen our digital capabilities – and we’re looking for an Enabling Engineer who wants to help teams succeed by removing friction, improving ways of working, and turning good ideas into reality.**

**This is a role for you who thrive at the intersection of technology, people, and collaboration – and who believe that the best solutions are built together.**

### 
**What you get**

At Lindex, you’re not just joining a fashion company, you’re becoming part of a company with a higher purpose, to drive meaningful change for women, and a team that truly cares about its people. When you join us, you’ll also enjoy:

* Supportive onboarding that helps you feel confident and ready
* A culture of collaboration, continuous learning and constructive feedback
* Generous 30 per cent staff discount
* Bonus programme based on our shared success
* Collective agreement
### **Your role in our team**


As an Enabling Engineer at Lindex Technology, your mission is to enable teams to work smarter, safer, and more efficiently. You act as a facilitator, problem-solver, and technical partner – helping teams adopt platforms, tools, and practices in a sustainable and scalable way.


You work closely with engineers, product teams, and stakeholders across the organisation, translating needs into practical solutions and ensuring that our technology landscape supports both current and future ways of working.


In the role you will also:

* Enable teams by improving developer experience, tooling, and workflows
* Support adoption of shared platforms, standards, and best practices
* Collaborate with teams to identify friction, bottlenecks, and improvement opportunities
* Contribute to automation, self-service, and platform capabilities
* Act as a bridge between technology, processes, and people
* Share knowledge through documentation, coaching, and hands-on support
### **Is this you?**


You are curious, collaborative, and genuinely enjoy helping others succeed. You take ownership of your development, feel comfortable navigating change, and like turning complex challenges into clear, usable solutions.


You welcome feedback, take initiative in your development, embrace learning, and are curious about digital tools that support you and your team to grow. We also believe you have:

* Experience working in engineering, platform, DevOps, or enabling roles
* A good understanding of modern cloud platforms, developer tools, or infrastructure
* Experience improving ways of working, processes, or technical enablement
* Strong communication skills and the ability to work with diverse teams
* Fluency in English, both spoken and written

Experience from retail, large organisations, or complex IT environments is a bonus – but your mindset and willingness to learn matter most.

### **Who we are**


We’re a growing global fashion company from Sweden, driven by a higher purpose: to drive meaningful change for women. Our commitment to women, sustainability, and customers guides everything we do as we build a brand-driven, sustainable company with a digital-first mindset. At Lindex, we’re all about doing things together – collaborating, sharing ideas, and supporting one another. Because together, we achieve more than we ever could alone.

### **Got a good feeling? We’d love to hear from you.**


Apply today – we’re excited to get to know you! If you have questions, reach out using the contact details below. We review applications continuously, so don’t wait too long. We like to keep things simple, so please apply online (not by email).

  


**Contact person:** Ulf Stjernberg


**Application deadline:** May 03, 2026


Apply now
company_description: Lindex är en finskägd modekedja inom Stockmann-gruppen och en modekedja i norra Europa

title: Senior AI Solutions Engineer
description:
**Om uppdraget**
----------------


Vi söker en Senior AI Solutions Engineer till ett spännande uppdrag i Göteborg. I rollen ingår du i ett specialiserat AI-team och ansvarar för att designa och implementera kompletta AI-lösningar som förbättrar befintliga produkter och operativa flöden. Uppdraget kräver närvaro på plats tre dagar per vecka.


Du arbetar längs hela livscykeln – från problemdefinition och modelldesign till driftsättning av fungerande lösningar – med hjälp av stora språkmodeller (LLM) i kombination med klassiska maskininlärningstekniker.

**Arbetsuppgifter**
-------------------

* Designa och implementera kompletta AI-lösningar från end-to-end
* Omvandla otydliga eller föränderliga krav till konkreta tekniska lösningar såsom modeller, datapipelines och beslutssystem
* Integrera AI-funktionalitet med data, verktyg och affärsprocesser
* Bygga och exponera tjänster (t.ex. API:er) för att tillgängliggöra AI-kapabiliteter i applikationer
* Kontinuerligt utvärdera och förfina lösningar baserat på verklig användning, begränsningar och avvägningar
* Samarbeta nära med produktägare och ingenjörer för att leverera fungerande lösningar
* Ta delat ansvar för hela DevOps- och MLOps-livscykeln

**Krav**
--------

* Minst fem (5) års erfarenhet av mjukvaruutveckling eller liknande roll
* Förmåga att självständigt designa och leverera kompletta end-to-end-lösningar med tydligt ägarskap över kvalitetsresultat
* Erfarenhet av maskininlärningsmetoder såsom regression, klassificering och tidsserieanalys
* Stark analytisk och problemlösande förmåga med ett praktiskt, ingenjörsinriktat synsätt
* Minst ett (1) års erfarenhet av AI-lösningar i produktionsmiljö
* Hög kompetensnivå i Python med förmåga att skapa och driftsätta enkla tjänster (t.ex. FastAPI)
* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av att göra LLM-baserade system tillförlitliga i verkliga miljöer, inklusive utvärderingsmetoder, felhantering, latens och kostnadsoptimering
* Gedigen erfarenhet på seniornivå inom tillämpad AI och maskininlärningsutveckling
* Praktisk bakgrund inom utveckling och drift av LLM-baserade lösningar i produktion, inklusive verktygsanvändning och flerstegsflöden

**Meriterande**
---------------

* Erfarenhet av containerisering, exempelvis Docker
* Kännedom om molnplattformar, särskilt Azure
* Erfarenhet av ostrukturerad eller komplex data från verkliga miljöer
* Tidigare exponering mot system på enterprisenivå eller operativa arbetsflöden

**Start:** 2026-05 **Längd:** 12 månader (möjlighet till förlängning) **Plats:** Göteborg (hybrid, 3 dagar/vecka på plats)

**Om Rasulson Consulting**
--------------------------


Rasulson Consulting är ett specialiserat bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag inom IT-sektorn. Vi samarbetar med både etablerade teknikbolag och innovativa startupföretag för att erbjuda attraktiva karriärmöjligheter för dig som vill utvecklas inom digitalisering och IT. Genom vår djupa tekniska kompetens och vårt omfattande nätverk matchar vi rätt konsult med rätt uppdrag – snabbt och träffsäkert. Hos oss får du personlig rådgivning, kontinuerlig återkoppling och möjlighet att ta nästa steg i din karriär.

title: Junior DevOps Engineer
description:
### **About FirstVet**


FirstVet is the first truly digital veterinary clinic. We let pet owners meet with licensed veterinarians via their mobile or computer — and we don't stop there. We've expanded to include an e-commerce platform, offering a comprehensive solution for pet owners across 7 markets in Europe and North America. Our mission is simple: to make quality veterinary care accessible to every pet and pet owner, wherever they are.


We're a tech-driven company at heart, and the engineering team plays a central role in how we deliver on that mission. If you're passionate about building reliable, scalable infrastructure and want to do it in a place where your work genuinely matters, we'd love to hear from you.

### **The role**


In this role, you will support our product teams in maintaining and continuously improving the infrastructure that underpins our services. You'll be joining a small, two-person DevOps team — which means real ownership and broad exposure across everything we do. While the DevOps team is small, you'll work closely with engineers across our four product teams, so collaboration and communication are a big part of the role. We're in an exciting phase where the business continues to grow and scale internationally, while at the same time we're focused on maturing and hardening the technological foundation we've built. Our current focus is on operational excellence: strengthening our security posture and ensuring our infrastructure is as robust and reliable as it can be. Alongside the day-to-day, we'd love for you to bring your own perspective — identifying gaps and proactively driving improvements.

### **Your responsibilities**

* Help maintain and improve the infrastructure, tooling, and delivery pipelines that allow our product teams to develop and operate their services in an efficient and independent way
* Contribute to our ongoing security and compliance work, building infrastructure that conforms to industry best practices
* Develop and improve our CI/CD pipelines as well as other automation tasks
* Maintain and evolve our shared infrastructure component library, internal CLI tooling and AI platform
* Drive initiatives to improve our observability foundation, enabling teams to better understand and operate their services
* Proactively identify areas for improvement and take ownership of driving them forward
* Share best practices and spread knowledge to other members of the team

### **The person we are looking for**


We are looking for a junior DevOps Engineer who is curious, collaborative, and genuinely excited about taking ownership. You don't need to have all the answers on day one — what matters more is that you're motivated to learn, you spot things that could be better, and you do something about them. You're comfortable supporting engineers across different teams and you understand the value of enabling others to work more independently. When you encounter a complex problem, you dig in, take ownership, and see it through to a solution.

### **Desired qualifications**

* Some hands-on experience with cloud infrastructure, ideally on AWS
* Familiarity with orchestration and containerization concepts such as Kubernetes, Docker, ECS or similar
* Experience with or exposure to CI/CD pipelines and an understanding of how they work
* Programming or scripting skills in a language such as TypeScript, Python, Bash or similar
* Experience with or a keen interest in infrastructure as code frameworks such as Terraform, Pulumi or similar
* A collaborative and communicative approach to working with engineers across teams
* Fluency in English, written and spoken

### **What we offer**


At FirstVet, you'll be joining a team that genuinely cares about what it's building and how it builds it. We value collaboration, trust, and giving people the space to do their best work. This role comes with real ownership from day one and plenty of room to grow. We offer a highly flexible, hybrid remote work environment — work from our Stockholm office or remotely, it's up to you. And of course, it goes without saying that an interest in animals is highly desirable. We're a pet-oriented organisation and it's not unusual to run into our office dogs or for a cat to join our Hangout calls!

title: Agile product leader
description:
We now have a new requirement and are looking for a consultant colleague with the qualifications listed below. The position is part of our consulting business, which means you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments.

*As part of your application, you may be presented to clients/partners as part of the recruitment process. If selected for the assignment, you will be employed by us and work either with clients or on internal projects and assignments as part of our consulting business.*

 **Your Profile (Must-have requirements)**  

* Proven experience as a Product Owner, Business Analyst, or in a similar role
* Experience working within ERP environments
* Hands-on experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365
* Experience in requirements management and process modeling
* Experience working in agile environments
* Strong understanding of end-to-end business processes, preferably within Order-to-Delivery
* Good understanding of system architecture and integration principles
* Ability to analyze data and track performance through KPIs

title: Product Owner - Microsoft Dynamics 365 - Automotive industry
description:
WeQuel is looking for an experienced Product Owner with deep expertise in Microsoft Dynamics 365 on behalf of one of our clients a global leader in the automotive industry. This is a high-impact assignment where you will own and drive the development of D365 functionality within a critical process area, ensuring that ERP solutions deliver real, measurable business value.


The role combines strategic product ownership with hands-on Business Analyst responsibilities. You will serve as the primary link between business stakeholders and development teams — translating complex operational needs into structured backlogs, functional specifications, and delivery roadmaps within the Order-to-Delivery domain.

**Key Responsibilities**


Product Ownership

* Own and manage the product vision and roadmap for D365 within the assigned domain
* Prioritize and maintain the product backlog based on business value, risk, and dependencies
* Define scope and make decisions on functional priorities
* Act as the primary interface between business stakeholders and development teams
* Ensure solutions are scalable and aligned with architectural standards


Business Analysis

* Analyze and document current and future-state business processes
* Elicit, structure, and translate requirements into user stories and functional specifications
* Facilitate workshops and requirements gathering sessions
* Support solution design with architects and development teams
* Ensure traceability between requirements, solutions, and business outcomes
* Contribute to test planning, UAT, and acceptance activities

**Required Qualifications**

* Proven experience as a Product Owner or Business Analyst in ERP environments
* Hands-on experience with Microsoft Dynamics 365
* Strong understanding of end-to-end business processes, preferably Order-to-Delivery
* Experience in requirements management and process modeling
* Solid background in agile delivery (Scrum, SAFe, or similar)
* Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills
* Fluent in English

**Meriting**

* Experience within manufacturing or automotive industry
* Familiarity with D365 F&O / Supply Chain Management modules
* Certifications such as PSPO, SAFe, or Microsoft Dynamics
* Experience working with Azure DevOps or JIRA
* Swedish language skills

**Assignment Details**

* Start: May 2026
* Duration: 12 months
* Location: Södertälje, Sweden
* Setup: Hybrid – 3 days/week on-site

**About WeQuel**


WeQuel is a Swedish consulting firm specializing in Engineering, Management, and Life Science. We place experienced consultants with leading organizations across Sweden, including clients in automotive, pharma, manufacturing, and technology.

title: Senior DevOps Engineer
description:
You know the feeling — you come across a job opening that feels like a perfect fit. You’re driven, you learn fast, and you deliver results — yet on paper, everyone seems to have the same CV. That’s exactly what’s broken in recruitment.


And that’s exactly what we fix.

### **Welcome to Assessio**


Assessio is a European leader in science-backed HR technology with a clear vision: Zero Talent Waste. For us this means unleashing the potential of employees and building a workplace where everyone can thrive.


Founded in Sweden in 1954, we’ve grown from a pioneer in psychometric assessments into a full-service HR technology provider. We combine decades of validated science with AI-supported insight to help you make better decisions across the employee lifecycle.


By connecting recruitment, development, performance, and professional recognition, we deliver people data you can trust. Protected by ISO-certified security, our tools provide valuable insights that drive real impact - helping businesses and institutions make fair, confident decisions about their workforces.

### **Your role**


As a Senior DevOps Engineer, you will play a central role in designing, implementing, and maintaining scalable, secure, and reliable cloud infrastructure and CI/CD processes. Your expertise will drive the automation, observability, and operational excellence that are at the heart of our growth. While your focus will be to work with European cloud providers, Github CI/CD, modern monitoring, and a microservices approach - you’ll also collaborate across the wider Assessio group, with opportunities to broaden your horizons in Kubernetes, AWS, and cutting-edge DevOps tooling.

### **What you'll do**

* Build, test, and maintain cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CDK, etc.), ensuring scalability, security, and reliability.
* Design, implement, and optimise modern CI/CD pipelines and automated deployment processes.
* Maintain platform reliability and performance by leveraging monitoring and alerting tools and supporting high availability and effective disaster recovery.
* Participate in application deployments and help manage databases and cloud resources.
* Collaborate closely with developers, product, and security teams to align infrastructure with business needs.
* Document infrastructure, automation workflows, and processes to promote transparency and knowledge sharing.
* Heavily leverage modern technologies, including GenAI and LLMs, to modernise and automate DevOps processes.

### **Who are you?**

* Bachelor's or master's degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field.
* Experience working with cloud services (Scaleway, AWS, or similar), including infrastructure setup and automation.
* Hands-on expertise in Infrastructure as Code (Terraform preferred), and CI/CD pipelines.
* Experience with containerisation and a good understanding of cloud data storage technologies.
* Understanding effective monitoring, alerting, and security principles.
* Proficient problem-solving skills, and ability to work both independently and collaboratively.
* Strong communication skills and experience in agile teams.
* Fluent in English.

### **Why Assessio?**


We are a fast-growing, international SaaS company with a clear ambition: to become the #1 player in Europe in the field of assessment and development.


We operate from offices in Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Estonia, Germany, and - since recently - France, but above all, we function as one global team.


Collaboration, growth, learning, and having fun together are at the heart of how we work.

### **We offer you**


A competitive salary with an individual commission model


️ A fully employer-funded pension plan


Focus and investment in your personal development


We offer a great office environment that you’ll enjoy working from, combined with the flexibility to work from home. When working remotely, you receive a home office allowance. Our guideline is three days in the office, depending on location.


The events committee regularly organises fun activities - think padel, bowling, or barbecues.


️ We’re an international company and value staying connected. That means a yearly company trip to Stockholm, Oslo, Copenhagen, Berlin, Paris or Amsterdam! Expect dinners, catching up with your international colleagues, inspiring sessions, and great parties.  

️ Every day, we work towards our mission: **Zero Talent Waste**. This is the goal behind everything we do. Contribute to a fair labor market - it adds an extra layer of meaning to your work.

 **Our hiring process**


We practice what we preach. We ask you to complete an assessment consisting of three parts: a cognitive ability assessment, a personality assessment, and a motivation questionnaire.


Everyone goes through this process. Why? To ensure that everyone gets a fair chance and that our decisions are based on **data**, not gut feeling. The assessment takes approximately **50 minutes**, and you’ll receive a quick response from us after completing it.


Want to learn more about the process? Visit our career page.  

Curious about the assessments? More information is available on our website (Aptitude and Personality)


We look forward to meeting you!


Apply now and help us shape the future of talent.

title: DevOps engineer Internship / LIA Praktik
description:

 Solna

**About The Role**
 We are looking for a motivated and curious DevOps Intern to join our platform engineering team. This internship is designed to give hands-on experience with modern DevOps practices, cloud infrastructure, and automation tools. You’ll work closely with experienced engineers to support development pipelines, improve system reliability, and streamline deployment processes.
   

  

 This is a great opportunity for someone eager to learn how software moves from development to production in a fast-paced, real-world environment.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Assist in building, maintaining, and improving CI/CD pipelines
* Support deployment and monitoring of applications in cloud environments
* Collaborate with developers to troubleshoot build and deployment issues
* Help automate repetitive tasks using scripts and DevOps tools

* Familiarity with at least one programming or scripting language (e.g., Python, Bash, JavaScript)

* Strong communication and teamwork skills
